key_insight: The paper develops an online algorithm for nonconvex, nonsmooth optimization where both losses and constraints are difference-of-convex compositions with smooth mappings, and proves a proximal-residual stationarity guarantee.
engineering_relevance: This provides a principled way to solve streaming constrained optimization problems using only convex subproblem oracles, which is useful for large-scale control, learning, and PDE-constrained systems.
method: Time-smoothed proximal linear updates for composite DC objectives
Local regret defined via a proximal residual mapping
Tangent-cone characterization of composite DC feasible sets
evidence_signal: The abstract reports theoretical guarantees: a local-regret bound, a bound on the total number of inner convex subproblems, and an error bound linking the residual to distance from stationarity.
watch_item: The guarantees are theoretical and rely on a convex-oracle inner loop plus problem structure; practical performance may depend on how accurately the composite DC models and smooth mappings capture the real system.
paper_title: Online Optimization of Difference-of-Convex Compositions with Smooth Mappings
